aliases: headache|sakit ulo|masakit ang ulo|masakit ulo|ulo ko|head pain|migraine|migrena|head ache|tension headache
tip_1: Rest in a quiet, dim room and start self-care as soon as symptoms begin.
tip_2: Drink water regularly — mild dehydration commonly worsens headache.
tip_3: A cool cloth on the forehead or gentle neck/shoulder stretches may help.
tip_4: If appropriate for you, use a labeled over-the-counter pain reliever exactly as directed; avoid using pain medicine most days of the week without advice.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care sooner if headache is sudden/severe, with neck stiffness, vision changes, weakness, confusion, vomiting, fever, or after a head injury.

aliases: fever|lagnat|hilanat|mainit ang lawas|high temperature|pyrexia|nagliinig|chills|gininig
tip_1: Rest and drink extra fluids (water, ORS, clear broths) to prevent dehydration.
tip_2: Wear light clothing and keep the room cool; a lukewarm sponge bath may help comfort after fever medicine if needed.
tip_3: Check temperature again after a few hours and note other symptoms.
tip_4: Adult fever medicine (as labeled) may be used for comfort if no allergy or medical warning; avoid aspirin in children and teens.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if fever is very high, lasts >3 days, with stiff neck, rash, breathing trouble, confusion, severe pain, seizure, or in infants/pregnant patients.

aliases: cough|ubo|nagaubo|dry cough|productive cough|hacking cough|night cough
tip_1: Sip warm caffeine-free fluids (water, tea, caldo) to soothe the throat and loosen mucus.
tip_2: Use honey in warm water for adults/older children if no allergy (not for infants under 1 year).
tip_3: Avoid smoke, strong scents, and dusty areas; prop your head with pillows at night if coughing interrupts sleep.
tip_4: OTC cough medicines are optional and not for young children — treat each symptom separately and follow age/label limits.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if cough lasts >2 weeks, with blood, chest pain, high fever, wheezing, or shortness of breath.

aliases: cold|colds|sipon|trangkaso|flu-like|runny nose|baradong ilong|stuffy nose|sneezing|bahing|sip-on|uri|upper respiratory
tip_1: Rest, sleep, and drink plenty of caffeine-free fluids to thin mucus.
tip_2: Saline nose spray/drops or brief steam (not scalding) can ease stuffiness.
tip_3: Blow the nose gently; wash hands often; sneeze into a tissue or elbow.
tip_4: Avoid leftover antibiotics — colds are usually viral; treat each symptom with labeled OTC options only if suitable for your age.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if symptoms worsen after about 10 days, or with difficulty breathing, sudden chest pain, severe vomiting, high fever, or strange/confused behavior.

aliases: sore throat|sakit sa tutunlan|masakit lalamunan|masakit ang lalamunan|tunlan|throat pain|pharyngitis
tip_1: Gargle with warm salt water several times a day if able.
tip_2: Drink warm fluids and avoid very hot, acidic, or spicy drinks.
tip_3: Use a humidifier or sit in a steamy bathroom briefly to moisten the throat.
tip_4: Rest your voice and consider lozenges/honey for adults if appropriate.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if you cannot swallow liquids, have drooling, high fever, rash, or breathing difficulty.

aliases: stomach ache|stomachache|sakit tiyan|masakit tiyan|sakit ang tiyan|abdominal pain|tiyan ko|gastro pain|gas pain
tip_1: Eat light bland foods (rice, banana, toast) and avoid greasy/spicy meals for a day.
tip_2: Sip water or ORS slowly; avoid large amounts of soda or coffee.
tip_3: Rest upright after meals; a warm compress on the abdomen may ease mild cramps.
tip_4: Do not take leftover prescription medicines without clinician advice.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for severe pain, persistent vomiting, blood in stool/vomit, rigid abdomen, pregnancy, or pain with fever.

aliases: diarrhea|diarrhoea|paglibang|taliq|nagaibot|loose stool|frequent bowel
tip_1: Start oral rehydration (ORS) or clear fluids frequently in small sips.
tip_2: Continue light foods when able; avoid oily, very sweet, or dairy-heavy meals temporarily.
tip_3: Wash hands after toilet use and before eating to prevent spread.
tip_4: OTC anti-diarrheal medicines are not advised if you have fever or bloody stools — ask a pharmacist/clinician first.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if diarrhea lasts >2 days in children or ~5 days in adults, with blood, high fever, severe pain, dehydration signs, or inability to keep fluids down.

aliases: constipation|matigas ang tae|hirap tumae|stool locked|walang tae
tip_1: Increase water intake through the day unless fluid-restricted by your clinician.
tip_2: Add fiber-rich foods (fruits, vegetables, whole grains) gradually.
tip_3: Stay lightly active (walking) to help bowel movement.
tip_4: Do not overuse strong laxatives; ask a pharmacist/clinician if needed.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for severe pain, vomiting, blood in stool, or no bowel movement for several days with swelling.

aliases: nausea|vomiting|sumerko|nagsusuka|ginagahab|gutom na sakit|queasy
tip_1: Sip clear fluids slowly (water, ORS, ice chips) rather than drinking quickly.
tip_2: Rest sitting upright; avoid strong smells and greasy foods.
tip_3: Try bland foods later (crackers, toast, rice) once vomiting settles.
tip_4: Avoid alcohol and new medicines unless advised.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if vomiting is persistent, with blood, severe abdominal pain, dehydration, or after a head injury.

aliases: dizziness|dizzy|lipong|nahihilo|hilo|lightheaded|vertigo mild
tip_1: Sit or lie down immediately until the spinning/lightheaded feeling passes.
tip_2: Stand up slowly; hold onto support when rising.
tip_3: Drink water and avoid skipping meals.
tip_4: Rest in a safe place and avoid driving or climbing while dizzy.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for sudden severe dizziness with chest pain, fainting, weakness, speech trouble, or after injury.

aliases: back pain|sakit likod|masakit ang likod|lumbar pain|lower back pain|baywang
tip_1: Avoid heavy lifting and sudden twisting for 24–48 hours.
tip_2: Use brief ice or warm compress packs as comfortably tolerated.
tip_3: Gentle walking and light stretching often help more than full bed rest.
tip_4: Keep good posture when sitting; support the lower back with a small pillow.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for back pain with leg weakness/numbness, loss of bladder/bowel control, fever, or trauma.

aliases: body ache|sakit lawas|masakit ang kalamnan|myalgia|muscle pain|pananakit ng katawan|pamamasa
tip_1: Rest sore muscles and avoid strenuous exercise until better.
tip_2: Apply a warm compress to tight muscle areas for comfort.
tip_3: Stay hydrated and get adequate sleep.
tip_4: Gentle stretching and slow walking may reduce stiffness.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if pain is severe after injury, with swelling/deformity, or with high fever.

aliases: joint pain|sakit tuhod|sakit kasukasuan|arthralgia|knee pain|ankle pain|shoulder pain
tip_1: Rest the joint and avoid activities that sharply increase pain.
tip_2: Elevate the area if swollen and consider a brief cold pack.
tip_3: Support with a soft wrap only if it does not worsen numbness/tingling.
tip_4: Continue light movement within comfort to prevent stiffness.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for hot swollen joints, inability to bear weight, deformity, or fever with joint pain.

aliases: toothache|sakit ngipin|masakit ang ngipin|dental pain|sakit bagang
tip_1: Rinse with warm salt water and keep the area clean.
tip_2: Avoid very hot, cold, or sugary foods that trigger pain.
tip_3: Use dental floss carefully to remove trapped food if present.
tip_4: OTC pain relief may help temporarily if suitable for you; it is not a cure.
when_to_seek_care: See a dentist promptly for swelling of the face, fever, pus, or pain lasting more than 1–2 days.

aliases: earache|sakit tenga|masakit ang tenga|ear pain|otitis mild
tip_1: Keep the ear dry; avoid inserting cotton buds or oils into the canal.
tip_2: Apply a warm (not hot) compress outside the ear for comfort.
tip_3: Rest with the painful ear up and take fluids.
tip_4: Do not share earphones while infected/irritated.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for severe pain, discharge, hearing loss, high fever, or symptoms after swimming/injury.

aliases: eye strain|nasakit mata|masakit ang mata|irritated eyes|red eye mild|mata ko|dry eyes
tip_1: Follow the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes look 20 feet away for 20 seconds.
tip_2: Blink often and reduce screen brightness if needed.
tip_3: Use preservative-free lubricating drops if recommended for dry eyes.
tip_4: Avoid rubbing the eyes and wash hands before touching them.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for sudden vision loss, severe pain, light sensitivity, chemical exposure, or injury.

aliases: allergy|allergies|allergic rhinitis|hapong|hapong-hapong|sneeze|bahing|itchy nose|pollen allergy|hay fever
tip_1: Rinse nasal passages with saline if available.
tip_2: Stay indoors when dust/pollen is high; keep windows closed if helpful.
tip_3: Wash bedding and avoid known triggers (dust, smoke, pet dander).
tip_4: Non-sedating antihistamines may help if appropriate and used as labeled.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for breathing difficulty, facial swelling, wheezing, or known severe allergy history.

aliases: rash|red spots|pantal|butol|skin rash|itchy rash mild|urticaria mild
tip_1: Keep the area clean and dry; avoid scratching to prevent infection.
tip_2: Use a gentle fragrance-free moisturizer.
tip_3: Wear loose cotton clothing over the irritated area.
tip_4: Cool compresses can reduce itch; ask a pharmacist about soothing lotions if needed.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for rapidly spreading rash, blistering, fever, facial/lip swelling, or breathing trouble.

aliases: insect bite|kagat ng lamok|kagat|mosquito bite|bug bite|ant bite
tip_1: Wash with soap and water, then apply a cool compress.
tip_2: Avoid scratching; short clean nails reduce skin breaks.
tip_3: Calamine or pharmacist-advised anti-itch cream may help.
tip_4: Watch for increasing redness, warmth, or pus.
when_to_seek_care: Seek urgent care for difficulty breathing, facial swelling, dizziness after a sting, or signs of serious allergic reaction.

aliases: cut|hiwa|gasgas|abrasion|minor wound|scraped skin|kalmot
tip_1: Wash gently with clean water and mild soap.
tip_2: Apply light pressure with a clean cloth if still bleeding.
tip_3: Cover with a clean bandage and change daily or when wet/dirty.
tip_4: Keep tetanus vaccination status in mind for deeper wounds.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for deep cuts, uncontrolled bleeding, numbness, animal bites, or signs of infection.

aliases: burn|paso|minor burn|scald|napaso
tip_1: Cool under cool running water for about 20 minutes (not ice).
tip_2: Remove tight items near the area early before swelling starts.
tip_3: Cover loosely with a clean non-stick dressing.
tip_4: Do not apply toothpaste, butter, or folk pastes.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for burns on face/hands/genitals, large areas, blisters that break open widely, or electrical/chemical burns.

aliases: fatigue|pagod|lupaypay|low energy|tiredness|panghihina|weakness mild
tip_1: Prioritize sleep (consistent bedtime) and short rest breaks in the day.
tip_2: Drink water and eat regular balanced meals; avoid skipping breakfast.
tip_3: Limit caffeine late in the day and reduce alcohol.
tip_4: Light outdoor walking can improve energy more than total inactivity.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if fatigue is sudden/severe, with chest pain, fainting, unexplained weight loss, or lasts many weeks.

aliases: insomnia|hirap matulog|di makatulog|sleepless|walang tulog
tip_1: Keep a regular sleep and wake time, including weekends when possible.
tip_2: Avoid screens and heavy meals close to bedtime.
tip_3: Make the room dark, quiet, and comfortably cool.
tip_4: Limit naps to under 30 minutes and not late afternoon.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if insomnia lasts weeks, with severe anxiety/depression, or daytime safety risks (falling asleep while driving).

aliases: menstrual pain|sakit regla|cramps|dysmenorrhea|period pain|masakit ang regla
tip_1: Use a warm compress on the lower abdomen.
tip_2: Gentle stretching, walking, or rest in a comfortable position may help.
tip_3: Stay hydrated and consider heat + OTC pain relief if suitable for you.
tip_4: Track cycle dates and symptom severity for your next visit.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for sudden severe pelvic pain, unusually heavy bleeding, fainting, pregnancy possibility, or pain not typical for you.

aliases: heartburn|asid|asido|acid reflux|maalsom|nagnaigas|burn chest mild|indigestion acid
tip_1: Eat smaller meals and avoid lying down for 2–3 hours after eating.
tip_2: Limit spicy, fatty, and highly acidic foods temporarily.
tip_3: Elevate the head of the bed slightly if night symptoms occur.
tip_4: Antacids may help short-term if appropriate and used as labeled.
when_to_seek_care: Seek urgent care for chest pressure with sweating, arm pain, shortness of breath, or first-time severe chest pain.

aliases: gas|kabag|bloated|kabag gas|flatulence|distended mild
tip_1: Eat slowly and avoid carbonated drinks for a day.
tip_2: Take a short walk after meals to help gas move.
tip_3: Reduce beans, cabbage, and heavy fried foods temporarily if they trigger you.
tip_4: Sip warm water or herbal tea if you tolerate it.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for severe progressive bloating with vomiting, no flatus/stool, or intense pain.

aliases: overuse|pagod katawan|masakit pagkatapos trabaho|strain mild|pulled muscle mild
tip_1: Use RICE basics: rest, ice briefly, compression if comfortable, elevation when swollen.
tip_2: Return to activity gradually; avoid sudden heavy loads.
tip_3: Gentle range-of-motion exercises within pain limits help recovery.
tip_4: Supportive footwear and good lifting posture prevent repeat strain.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if you heard a pop, cannot move the limb, have numbness, or swelling worsens quickly.

aliases: uti mild|masakit when peeing|ardor al orinar|sakit ihi|burning urination mild|frequent urination mild
tip_1: Drink extra water unless advised to restrict fluids.
tip_2: Do not delay urination; empty the bladder fully.
tip_3: Avoid irritants (alcohol, caffeine, very spicy foods) for a day or two.
tip_4: Keep genital area clean and dry; wipe front to back.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care promptly for fever, back/flank pain, blood in urine, pregnancy, male patients with symptoms, or symptoms lasting >1–2 days.

aliases: hemorrhoid mild|piles|masakit puwit mild|almoranas mild
tip_1: Increase fiber and water to soften stools and reduce straining.
tip_2: Avoid sitting for very long periods; take short standing breaks.
tip_3: Warm sitz baths can soothe local irritation.
tip_4: Keep the area clean with water; pat dry gently.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for heavy bleeding, severe pain, prolapsed tissue, or dizziness from blood loss.

aliases: stress|kabalaka|anxiety mild|tense|kabado|worried mild|panic mild not emergency
tip_1: Practice slow breathing: inhale 4 seconds, exhale 6 seconds, for a few minutes.
tip_2: Take a short walk or stretch break away from the stressor.
tip_3: Talk with a trusted person and keep a simple worry list to revisit later.
tip_4: Protect sleep, meals, and hydration — basic routines reduce stress intensity.
when_to_seek_care: Seek urgent help for thoughts of self-harm, inability to function, or panic with chest pain/shortness of breath that feels new or severe.

aliases: dehydration mild|uhaw|tuyong bibig|dry mouth|konting ihi
tip_1: Drink ORS or water regularly in small amounts rather than chugging once.
tip_2: Replace fluids after heat exposure, diarrhea, or heavy sweating.
tip_3: Rest in a cool shaded place.
tip_4: Eat watery fruits (banana, melon) when appetite returns.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for confusion, fainting, no urine for many hours, or inability to keep fluids down.

aliases: motion sickness|sasakyan|hilo sa byahe|seasick
tip_1: Sit where motion is least (front seat / over wings) and look at the horizon.
tip_2: Avoid heavy meals and strong smells before travel.
tip_3: Fresh air and limiting screen use while moving can help.
tip_4: Ask a pharmacist about travel tablets if appropriate for you.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if vomiting is severe with headache/neck stiffness or neurological symptoms.

aliases: sinus|sinusitis|sinus infection|masakit sinuses|pressure sa mukha|facial pressure|congested sinuses|sipon na matagal
tip_1: Drink clear fluids often to keep mucus thin so sinuses can drain.
tip_2: Apply a warm moist cloth to the face several times a day; breathe mild steam (bathroom shower steam — not scalding).
tip_3: Use saline nasal spray several times daily; a clean humidifier can help indoor air.
tip_4: If using OTC decongestant nasal spray, limit to a few days only — longer use can rebound congestion.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if symptoms last >10–14 days, severe headache not eased by pain medicine, fever, vision changes, or facial swelling.

aliases: nosebleed|epistaxis|dugo sa ilong|pagdurugo ng ilong|bleeding nose|nullos dugo
tip_1: Sit up, lean slightly forward, and breathe through your mouth — do not tip the head back.
tip_2: Pinch the soft part of both nostrils firmly for a full 10–15 minutes without peeking.
tip_3: You may place a cool compress across the bridge of the nose; avoid packing gauze deep inside.
tip_4: After it stops, avoid blowing/picking the nose and heavy lifting for several hours; saline gel can reduce dryness later.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if bleeding continues after two full pressure attempts, follows a head injury, or you feel faint/weak, or nosebleeds keep recurring.

aliases: sprain|sprained ankle|nabaligtad paa|nastrain|twisted ankle|sakit bukong-bukong|mild sprain
tip_1: Rest the injured area and avoid putting full weight on it for 1–2 days if painful.
tip_2: Ice 15–20 minutes at a time (cloth barrier) several times a day during the first 48 hours.
tip_3: Use a snug elastic wrap for light compression if toes stay warm/pink and sensation is normal.
tip_4: Elevate the limb above heart level when possible to limit swelling.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if you cannot bear weight, the joint feels unstable/numb, bone pain is severe, or it is not improving after 2–3 days.

aliases: neck pain|sakit leeg|masakit ang leeg|stiff neck mild|torticolis mild
tip_1: Avoid sudden twisting and prolonged phone/computer strain; keep screens at eye level.
tip_2: Apply a warm compress or brief ice pack as comfortably tolerated.
tip_3: Gentle range-of-motion stretches within pain limits often help more than complete immobility.
tip_4: Support the neck with a supportive pillow; avoid stomach sleeping if it worsens pain.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for neck pain with fever, severe headache, weakness/numbness in arms, or after a fall/collision.

aliases: sunburn|paso ng araw|nasunog sa araw|red skin from sun
tip_1: Get out of the sun and cool the skin with lukewarm water or a cool compress (not ice directly).
tip_2: Moisturize with a gentle lotion; avoid petroleum-heavy greasy products on fresh burns if they trap heat.
tip_3: Drink extra water; loose cotton clothing reduces friction.
tip_4: OTC pain relief may help discomfort if suitable for you; do not pop large blisters.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for extensive blistering, fever, confusion, severe pain, or burns on face/eyes/genitals.

aliases: indigestion|dyspepsia|sakit dibdib after kain|busog na masakit|upset stomach|maalsom tiyan
tip_1: Eat smaller slower meals; stop eating when comfortably full.
tip_2: Avoid trigger foods (very fatty, spicy, fried) and limit alcohol/caffeine for a day.
tip_3: Stay upright after eating; a short walk can ease fullness.
tip_4: Antacids may help short-term if appropriate and labeled for you.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for vomiting blood, black stools, unintentional weight loss, or chest pain that could be cardiac.

aliases: flu|influenza|trangkaso malala mild|body flu|flu symptoms
tip_1: Rest at home and sleep more while contagious symptoms are active.
tip_2: Drink fluids often; use fever/pain medicine only as labeled if needed for comfort.
tip_3: Isolate from higher-risk household members when possible; cover coughs and wash hands.
tip_4: Antibiotics do not treat flu viruses — ask about testing/antivirals early if you are high risk.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for trouble breathing, chest pain, confusion, inability to keep fluids down, or symptoms that suddenly worsen.

aliases: mouth ulcer|canker sore|singaw|sakit sa bibig|aphthous ulcer
tip_1: Rinse with warm salt water and avoid acidic/spicy foods that sting.
tip_2: Use a soft toothbrush and keep the mouth clean.
tip_3: Cool soft foods and chilled drinks may feel better.
tip_4: Ask a pharmacist about soothing gels if ulcers are very painful.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if ulcers last >2 weeks, you cannot eat/drink, have high fever, or many ulcers appear suddenly.

aliases: hoarse|paos|nawala boses|laryngitis|lost voice|masakit boses
tip_1: Rest your voice — avoid whispering (it can strain more); speak softly only when needed.
tip_2: Drink warm fluids and humidify the air.
tip_3: Avoid smoke and throat clearing as much as possible.
tip_4: Honey/lozenges may soothe adults if appropriate.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if hoarseness lasts >2–3 weeks, or you have breathing difficulty, coughing blood, or unexplained weight loss.

aliases: pink eye|conjunctivitis|namumula mata|sore eyes mild|mata na may sipon
tip_1: Wash hands often and avoid touching/rubbing the eyes.
tip_2: Do not share towels, pillows, or eye makeup.
tip_3: Cool clean compresses can ease discomfort; discard contact lenses until cleared.
tip_4: Artificial tears may soothe irritation if recommended for you.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for vision loss, severe pain, light sensitivity, eye injury, or symptoms in a newborn.

aliases: bruise|pasa|black and blue|contusion mild|nagusmok
tip_1: Rest the area and elevate if on a limb to limit swelling.
tip_2: Apply a cool pack with a cloth barrier for brief periods in the first day.
tip_3: After 1–2 days, warm compresses may help fading comfort for some people.
tip_4: Avoid massage of a fresh painful bruise.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for bruises after a major fall/blow to the head, unexplained easy bruising, or swelling that expands quickly.

aliases: heat exhaustion mild|init|heat stress|sobra ang init|init ng araw|nauuinit
tip_1: Move to a cool shaded or air-conditioned place right away.
tip_2: Loosen clothing and apply cool wet cloths to the skin.
tip_3: Sip water or ORS slowly if you are fully awake and not vomiting heavily.
tip_4: Rest until sweating and weakness improve.
when_to_seek_care: Seek urgent care for confusion, fainting, vomiting that prevents drinking, very hot dry skin, or seizures — possible heat emergency.

aliases: leg cramp|pulikat|charley horse|sakit binti bigla|calf cramp
tip_1: Gently stretch and massage the cramped muscle; walk carefully once it eases.
tip_2: Hydrate and ensure you are not skipping meals/electrolytes after heavy sweat.
tip_3: Apply a warm pack after the cramp settles if the muscle remains tight.
tip_4: Review sudden new medicines with a clinician/pharmacist if cramps are frequent.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for swelling/redness in one leg, chest pain, or cramps with severe weakness.

aliases: morning sickness|nausea pregnancy mild|sumerko buntis|pregnancy nausea mild
tip_1: Eat small frequent bland snacks (crackers) rather than large meals.
tip_2: Sip fluids between meals; ginger tea or hard ginger candy may help some people.
tip_3: Avoid strong smells and greasy foods.
tip_4: Rest and rise slowly from bed.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for inability to keep fluids down, dizziness, weight loss, severe vomiting, or abdominal pain — do not start new medicines without clinician advice.

aliases: food poisoning|sira ang tiyan|kuspad|foodborne mild|agusaw after kain
tip_1: Focus on ORS/clear fluids first until vomiting settles.
tip_2: Return to bland foods gradually (BRAT-style: banana, rice, apple sauce, toast) when hungry.
tip_3: Rest and avoid dairy, alcohol, and greasy foods for a day or two.
tip_4: Practice careful handwashing and discard leftover suspect food.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for bloody diarrhea, high fever, severe dehydration, or symptoms lasting beyond a couple of days.

aliases: athlete foot|alipunga|itchy feet fungus|foot fungus mild
tip_1: Wash and dry feet carefully, especially between toes.
tip_2: Wear clean dry socks; alternate shoes to let them dry.
tip_3: Over-the-counter antifungal cream may help if used as labeled for the full course.
tip_4: Avoid sharing towels and walking barefoot in damp public areas when possible.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if the rash spreads, has pus, severe swelling, fever, or you have diabetes/poor circulation.

aliases: cold sore|herpes labialis|singaw sa labi|fever blister
tip_1: Avoid kissing/sharing utensils while sores are open.
tip_2: Apply a cool compress; keep the area clean and dry.
tip_3: Ask a pharmacist about topical antiviral creams started early if appropriate.
tip_4: Do not pick the sore; wash hands after touching it.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for sores near the eyes, frequent severe outbreaks, or sores with high fever in infants.

aliases: post nasal drip|uhog sa lalamunan|throat mucus|tickle throat mucus
tip_1: Drink fluids and use saline nasal rinse/spray to thin mucus.
tip_2: Sleep with the head slightly elevated.
tip_3: A clean humidifier may reduce throat tickle from dryness.
tip_4: Avoid smoke; treat allergy triggers if they are known.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if drip lasts weeks with facial pain, green/bloody discharge, fever, or wheezing.

aliases: chest congestion|phlegm sa dibdib|malapot uhog|productive chest mild
tip_1: Drink warm fluids and rest to help loosen phlegm.
tip_2: Use steamy air carefully; avoid smoke exposure.
tip_3: Honey (adults/older children) may soothe cough; expectorants only if labeled for you.
tip_4: Do not suppress a productive cough completely if mucus is clearing.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for shortness of breath, chest pain, bluish lips, high fever, or coughing blood.

aliases: sciatica|sakit nerbiyos binti|shooting leg pain mild|nerve pain leg mild
tip_1: Avoid prolonged sitting; take short walking breaks.
tip_2: Use ice or heat packs as comfortable for short sessions.
tip_3: Gentle stretches for hips/hamstrings within pain limits may help.
tip_4: Sleep on a supportive surface; a pillow between knees can reduce strain.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for progressive weakness, numbness in the groin, or loss of bladder/bowel control.

aliases: wrist pain|sakit pulso|carpal strain mild|nastrain kamay
tip_1: Rest from repetitive straining tasks for 24–48 hours.
tip_2: Ice briefly with a cloth barrier if swollen.
tip_3: A soft wrist support may help daytime tasks if sensation stays normal.
tip_4: Return to typing/lifting gradually with neutral wrist posture.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care after a fall onto the wrist, visible deformity, or numbness that does not improve.

aliases: heel pain|sakit sakong|plantar fasciitis mild|sakit talampakan
tip_1: Reduce high-impact activity for a few days; choose supportive shoes.
tip_2: Gentle calf stretches before standing first thing in the morning may help.
tip_3: Ice the heel briefly after activity if sore.
tip_4: Avoid walking barefoot on hard floors while symptomatic.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for inability to walk, heel bruising after injury, or pain that worsens over weeks.

aliases: dry skin|eczema mild|pagkatuyo ng balat|itchy dry skin|kaliskis balat mild
tip_1: Moisturize often with fragrance-free cream after bathing.
tip_2: Use lukewarm (not hot) water and gentle cleanser; pat dry.
tip_3: Wear soft cotton layers; avoid known irritants (harsh soaps, wool if triggering).
tip_4: Keep nails short to reduce scratch damage.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for oozing infection, fever, rapidly spreading redness, or severe sleep-disturbing itch.

aliases: dengue|dengue watch|dengue mild watch|possible dengue home|lagnat dengue watch|dengge
display_name: Fever with body pain (dengue-watch home care)
tip_1: Rest and drink ORS/water frequently; monitor urine output.
tip_2: Use acetaminophen/paracetamol only as labeled for fever/pain if suitable — avoid aspirin/ibuprofen if dengue is possible unless a clinician says otherwise.
tip_3: Use mosquito nets/repellent to protect others; eliminate standing water around the home.
tip_4: Watch warning signs closely: severe belly pain, persistent vomiting, bleeding gums/nose, black stools, restlessness, cold clammy skin.
when_to_seek_care: Seek urgent care for any warning signs, fever with bleeding, severe lethargy, or inability to drink — dengue can worsen quickly after the fever drops.

aliases: covid mild|covid-19 home|positive covid mild|corona mild
tip_1: Rest at home, isolate as advised locally, and improve room ventilation when possible.
tip_2: Drink fluids; use labeled fever/pain medicine for comfort if appropriate.
tip_3: Monitor breathing and oxygen needs if you have a pulse oximeter and know your baseline.
tip_4: Contact a clinician early if you are high risk — antivirals work best soon after symptoms start.
when_to_seek_care: Seek urgent care for trouble breathing, chest pain, confusion, blue lips, or inability to stay awake.

aliases: sore arm after vaccine|baka pain|vaccine side effect mild|masakit braso bakuna
tip_1: Move the arm gently through normal range several times a day.
tip_2: A cool compress can ease local soreness.
tip_3: Labeled OTC pain relievers may help if suitable for you.
tip_4: Rest if you feel briefly fatigued; stay hydrated.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for rash with breathing difficulty, high fever lasting days, or arm swelling that rapidly worsens.

aliases: diaper rash|lampin|pulang puwet bata|nappy rash
tip_1: Change wet/soiled diapers promptly and clean gently with water.
tip_2: Pat dry and allow short diaper-free air time when practical.
tip_3: Use a thick barrier cream (zinc oxide style) as labeled.
tip_4: Avoid harsh wipes with alcohol/fragrance if they irritate.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for open sores, fever, spreading redness, or rash lasting more than a few days.

aliases: teething|ngingipin|baby teething|sakit gilagid bata
tip_1: Offer a clean cool (not frozen hard) teething ring if age-appropriate.
tip_2: Gently massage the gum with a clean finger.
tip_3: Keep the face dry; drool can irritate the chin — apply a thin barrier cream if needed.
tip_4: Avoid teething gels with benzocaine in young children unless a clinician advises.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for high fever, rash, diarrhea with dehydration, or refusal to drink — teething alone rarely causes high fever.

aliases: hiccups|singultus|solok-solok|hikab hiccup
tip_1: Sip cold water slowly or hold a brief breath, then exhale gently.
tip_2: Breathe into a paper bag briefly only if safe and not for people with breathing disease.
tip_3: Relax the diaphragm; avoid carbonated drinks and rapid eating for a while.
tip_4: Most hiccup bouts stop on their own within minutes to hours.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if hiccups last >48 hours, interfere with sleep/eating, or follow recent surgery/illness.

aliases: splinter|tinik|aspili sa balat|tuhog kahoy
tip_1: Wash hands and the area with soap and water.
tip_2: Use cleaned tweezers to remove a visible splinter along the entry track.
tip_3: If the tip is under skin, a sterilized needle may help lift it — then wash again.
tip_4: Apply a clean bandage; watch for redness.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if the splinter is deep, under a nail, from rusty/dirty metal, or infection signs appear.

aliases: blister|paltos|blister from shoes|paltos paa
tip_1: Leave intact blisters closed when possible — the skin is a natural bandage.
tip_2: Cover with a clean blister plaster or soft padding to reduce rub.
tip_3: If a blister opens, wash gently and cover with a non-stick dressing.
tip_4: Wear better-fitting socks/shoes to prevent repeat blisters.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for spreading redness, pus, fever, or blisters in people with diabetes/poor circulation.

aliases: loss of appetite|walang gana|reduced appetite mild|ayaw kumain mild
tip_1: Offer small frequent easy meals rather than large plates.
tip_2: Choose nutrient-dense soft foods and keep fluids up.
tip_3: Light activity and fresh air before meals may help appetite.
tip_4: Avoid forcing large meals; track weight if appetite stays low.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care if appetite loss lasts weeks, with weight loss, fever, vomiting, or severe fatigue.

aliases: gout mild|sakit daliri paa bigla|gouty toe mild|uric acid pain mild
tip_1: Rest the joint and elevate it; avoid pressure from tight shoes.
tip_2: Ice briefly with a cloth barrier for comfort.
tip_3: Drink water unless fluid-restricted; avoid alcohol during a flare.
tip_4: Do not start new strong pain medicines without advice if kidney disease or ulcers are known.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for hot swollen joint with fever, first-time severe attack, or inability to walk.

aliases: itchy eyes allergy|hapong mata|watery allergic eyes|allergic conjunctivitis mild
tip_1: Avoid rubbing; use cool compresses.
tip_2: Reduce exposure to dust/pollen; wash face and change pillowcases.
tip_3: Preservative-free lubricating drops may soothe; ask about antihistamine eye drops if needed.
tip_4: Wash hands before any eye drop use.
when_to_seek_care: Seek care for pain, vision change, thick yellow discharge, or light sensitivity.

aliases: pinworm|bulate sa puwit|anal itch night|enterobius mild
tip_1: Bathe in the morning and change underwear daily.
tip_2: Wash hands after toilet and before meals; keep nails short.
tip_3: Wash bedding/pajamas frequently in hot water when possible.
tip_4: Do not share towels; clean bathroom surfaces.
when_to_seek_care: See a clinician for confirmed treatment — self-medicating worm tablets without advice is not recommended for all ages.

aliases: general|non urgent|mild symptoms|not sure
tip_1: Rest, drink water, and monitor how you feel over the next 24–48 hours.
tip_2: Write down when symptoms started and what makes them better or worse.
tip_3: Use only labeled OTC remedies appropriate for your age/health conditions.
tip_4: Avoid sharing medicines prescribed for someone else.
when_to_seek_care: Book a consultation if symptoms persist, worsen, or you become unsure — you may choose to see a licensed doctor anytime.
